title: Selected Projects
note: Derived view of headline projects from experience.json, shaped for a card/detail UI. experience.json remains the source of truth for bullets.
intro: Production AI systems across drug safety, oncology, clinical imaging, and enterprise vision — selected for measurable impact, technical depth, and scale of deployment.

projects.name: Drug Safety AI Platform
projects.summary: An AI platform that predicts drug safety at scale by learning from omics, imaging, and molecular data together — delivered within a $3M+ SVP-sponsored initiative.
projects.problem: Drug safety assessment relies on costly animal studies and multi-year timelines, limiting compound prioritization at scale.
projects.solution: Built a model-agnostic multimodal AI platform integrating omics, imaging, and molecular features with productionized ML pipelines.
projects.architecture: Online learning, confidence estimation, multimodal augmentation, and joint embedding across heterogeneous biological data sources.
projects.businessImpact: Projected $20M–$50M annual savings by reducing animal studies and compressing safety timelines from five to three years.
projects.outcome: Platform productionized within an SVP-sponsored initiative; Broad Institute collaboration on Cell Painting and omics pipelines.
projects.lessons: Cross-vendor data integration and stakeholder alignment are as critical as model architecture for enterprise drug-safety AI.
highlights: Projected $20M–$50M annual savings by reducing animal studies and compressing safety timelines from five to three years.
Productionized ML pipelines with online learning, confidence estimation, and joint embedding.
Broad Institute collaboration on Cell Painting and omics pipelines.

projects.name: Tumor Recurrence Prediction
projects.summary: A tumor-recurrence prediction pipeline that learns from single-cell transcriptomics and holds up across different drugs, treatment conditions, and data sources — part of a Chief Scientist-sponsored oncology initiative.
projects.problem: Predicting tumor recurrence across heterogeneous data sources and treatment conditions remains a major translational oncology challenge.
projects.solution: Engineered a single-cell transcriptomics pipeline enriched with Med-PaLM LLM embeddings and knowledge graph representations.
projects.architecture: Generalizable pipeline across organoids, xenografts, and clinical studies with Optuna-driven hyperparameter optimization.
projects.businessImpact: Chief Scientist-sponsored initiative advancing AstraZeneca oncology R&D priority areas.
projects.outcome: 90% F1 on internal benchmarks; 13%+ improvement over state-of-the-art on public datasets; 30% compute time reduction.
projects.lessons: LLM embeddings and knowledge graphs can materially improve signal quality when raw transcriptomics alone is insufficient.
highlights: 90% F1 on internal benchmarks across Osimertinib-treated cell lines; 13%+ over state-of-the-art on public datasets.
Integrated Med-PaLM LLM embeddings and AZ Biological Insights Knowledge Graph representations.
Reduced pipeline compute time 30% via Optuna-driven hyperparameter search.

projects.name: Foundation Model Framework
projects.summary: A reusable framework that gives teams one standard way to train, run, and evaluate vision foundation models — scaled across CT, endoscopy, and echocardiography.
projects.problem: Each new clinical imaging use case required bespoke ML infrastructure, slowing experimentation and deployment.
projects.solution: Architected a reusable cross-platform framework standardizing training, inference, and evaluation of vision foundation models.
projects.architecture: Standardized pipelines deployed across 3 clinical imaging modalities and 3 internal AZ infrastructures.
projects.businessImpact: Accelerated onboarding of new imaging use cases; endoscopy model deployed in a clinical study for tissue damage severity ranking.
projects.outcome: Framework scaled across CT, Endoscopy, and Echocardiography with rapid experimentation and deployment capability.
projects.lessons: Platform thinking — not project thinking — is essential for scaling foundation models across modalities.
highlights: Deployed across three clinical imaging modalities and three internal AZ infrastructures.
Accelerated onboarding of new imaging use cases for rapid experimentation and deployment.
Endoscopy model deployed in a clinical study ranking gastrointestinal tissue damage severity.

projects.name: CT Tumor Segmentation
projects.summary: Next-generation CT tumor segmentation pipeline delivered in 6 months, with Dice scores 46% above state-of-the-art, enabling $0.5M in follow-on funding.
projects.problem: NSCLC tumor segmentation benchmarks required significant improvement to unlock follow-on funding and clinical utility.
projects.solution: Radiologist-inspired dynamic window function and test-time augmentation in a distributed training pipeline.
projects.architecture: Model and data parallelism reducing training time 20% and GPU memory 15–20%.
projects.businessImpact: Enabled $0.5M in follow-on funding; exceeded stakeholder targets by 4–5x on NSCLC benchmarks.
projects.outcome: Dice scores 46% above state-of-the-art; validated across independent clinical trial datasets including POSEIDON.
highlights: Radiologist-inspired dynamic window function and test-time augmentation improved Dice by 11% and 7%.
Exceeded stakeholder targets by 4–5x on NSCLC tumor segmentation benchmarks.
Reduced training time 20% and GPU memory 15-20% via model and data parallelism.

projects.name: Foundation Models for Digital Pathology
projects.summary: Scalable, reusable histopathology foundation model platform on AWS, delivering contrastive and inpainting model variants at 60% lower GPU compute cost.
projects.problem: Histopathology AI required expensive GPU compute and lacked reusable infrastructure for pathologist annotation workflows.
projects.solution: End-to-end AWS ML pipeline with contrastive and inpainting foundation model variants and GxP-compliant annotation tooling.
projects.architecture: Distributed training, model interpretability, and inference on AWS with Label Studio for pathologists.
projects.businessImpact: Delivered 2 model variants at 60% lower GPU compute cost; matched state-of-the-art benchmark performance.
projects.outcome: Led a 4-member team; platform adopted for internal AI-Bench environment.
highlights: End-to-end AWS ML pipeline: ingestion, preprocessing, distributed training, interpretability, inference.
Contrastive foundation models matched state-of-the-art at reduced GPU compute cost.
GxP-compliant Label Studio annotation tooling for pathologists; led a four-member team.

projects.name: Graph Neural Networks & Clinical Imaging
projects.summary: GNN-based pipeline for survival and response prediction on multiplex immunofluorescence histopathology slides, mapping the tumor microenvironment.
projects.problem: Multiplex immunofluorescence images contain rich spatial biology that traditional CNNs fail to capture for response prediction.
projects.solution: Graph-embedding pipelines mapping the tumor microenvironment across NSCLC and HNSCC cohorts.
projects.architecture: Modular preprocessing extracting graphs from multispectral gigapixel histopathology images.
projects.businessImpact: Published in JITC 2021; advanced computational pathology capabilities for solid-tumor response prediction.
projects.outcome: Graph-embedding pipelines deployed across NSCLC and HNSCC solid-tumor cohorts.
highlights: Graph-embedding pipelines across NSCLC and HNSCC solid-tumor cohorts.
Modular preprocessing extracting graphs from multispectral gigapixel histopathology images.

projects.name: Enterprise Vision Analytics
projects.summary: Production face, video, and pose analytics platforms — real-time recognition, person re-identification, multi-object tracking, and behavior analysis across enterprise deployments.
projects.problem: Enterprise clients needed production-grade computer vision across face, video, and pose analytics in resource-constrained environments.
projects.solution: Modular Docker-based deployment with edge-optimized model compression and scalable pipelines.
projects.architecture: Real-time recognition, ReID, MOT, and pose estimation with TensorFlow-based spatiotemporal features.
projects.businessImpact: Deployed across 2 client environments with accelerated use-case onboarding.
projects.outcome: Production systems for face recognition, ReID/MOT, and multi-environment pose estimation.
highlights: Real-time face recognition, verification, and identity clustering across two client deployments.
ReID and MOT pipelines robust to lighting, camera motion, and multi-scene conditions.
Edge-optimized model compression and Docker-based modular deployment.

projects.name: Clinical Imaging Systems
projects.summary: Real-time clinical imaging systems — 4K neonatal retinal imaging, GPU endoscopy with digital NBI, mouse-brain 3D reconstruction, and ultrasound-guided robotic navigation.
projects.problem: Clinical imaging devices required real-time GPU processing and novel algorithm development across multiple modalities.
projects.solution: Digital NBI pipeline, real-time endoscopy on Jetson TX1, and 3D neuroanatomy reconstruction algorithms.
projects.architecture: 10+ integrated modules on NVIDIA Jetson TX1; neuron-connectivity toolbox for CSHL and RIKEN.
projects.businessImpact: Digital NBI eliminated dedicated hardware, cutting ~$5K per device; toolbox delivered to Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory.
projects.outcome: First-of-its-kind 4K neonatal retinal imaging pipeline; real-time endoscopy system with polyp detection and enhancement modules.
highlights: Digital NBI pipeline eliminated dedicated hardware, cutting ~$5K per device.
Real-time endoscopy system with 10+ modules on NVIDIA Jetson TX1.
Neuron-connectivity toolbox delivered to Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory and RIKEN.
